_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-26 Interfacility High Flow Nasal Oxygen (Revised 3/3/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-26: Interfacility High-Flow Nasal Oxygen — MDHHS Approved 3/3/2025
purpose: PARAMEDIC USE ONLY. MCA OPTIONAL. High-flow nasal oxygen (HFNO) is authorized for interfacility transfers only — it is NOT a standalone protocol; MCA must also select it on the Interfacility Patient Transfers Protocol. Used for hypoxic respiratory failure with a sending facility order. Pediatric use requires additional MCA staffing selection.

sections.title: Authorization Requirements
points: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has selected HFNO in both this protocol AND the Interfacility Patient Transfers Protocol
PEDIATRIC MCA SELECTION required separately — options: facility staff must accompany / Enhanced Paramedic or Critical Care Paramedic only / Paramedic with MCA-approved HFNO training

sections.title: Indications
points: Sending facility order for HFNO is present
Patient has hypoxic respiratory failure, hypoxic respiratory distress, or respiratory distress
MCA-approved HFNO device and necessary supplies are available to facilitate transport

sections.title: Administration
points: SpO2 target: ≥ 94% or the patient's targeted baseline oxygen saturation as directed by the sending physician
Use facility HFNO settings as the starting point when available
— Patients > 12 kg: (2 L/kg/min × 12 kg) + (0.5 L/kg/min × [weight in kg − 12 kg])
Vital signs documented at minimum every 5 minutes
Consider escalation if patient is on > 2 L/kg/min or at maximum device settings

sections.title: Deterioration and Discontinuation
points: If patient deteriorates on HFNO: terminate HFNO and escalate to CPAP, BiPAP, BVM, or intubation per appropriate protocol
COVID-19 patients on HFNO: full PPE (N95 or higher respirator, face shield, gown, gloves) required
Congenital heart disease patients: may have a lower baseline SpO2 — consult with sending facility regarding target SpO2

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must also select HFNO authorization on the Interfacility Patient Transfers Protocol in addition to this selection
options: Authorized — Paramedic with appropriate HFNO training

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select the staffing requirement for pediatric patients on HFNO during interfacility transport
options: Sending facility staff must accompany patient
Enhanced Paramedic or Critical Care Paramedic only

deterioration_flags: SpO2 below target on HFNO — reassess flow rate; escalate to CPAP/BiPAP/BVM/intubation if not responsive
Patient at maximum device settings — consider escalation of care; contact Medical Control
COVID-19 or suspected aerosol-generating condition — ensure full PPE before HFNO
scope_notes: Requires MCA selection in BOTH this protocol AND Interfacility Patient Transfers Protocol
Interfacility transfers only — not for 9-1-1 primary responses
SpO2 target ≥ 94% or patient baseline target directed by sending physician
Flow: 2 L/kg/min up to 12 kg; + 0.5 L/kg/min per kg above 12 kg; max 60 L/min
Pediatric: additional MCA staffing selection required
